economic_notes: First Nation is heavily dependent on Business Enterprises (Fort McKay Group of Companies LP and Fort McKay Landing LP) involved in oil sands services and development. Business enterprises had aggregate revenue of $427,286,860 and net income of $52,847,051 in 2022. First Nation also holds a Settlement Trust (Heritage Fund from Treaty Entitlement Settlement) valued at $68,334,809. Committed to invest additional $11,700,000 into Northleaf investment funds.
notes: Fiscal year end is March 31, 2022 (not 2024). Business enterprises (Fort McKay Group of Companies LP and Fort McKay Landing LP) are accounted for using the modified equity method as GBEs, not fully consolidated. Business enterprise equity earnings of $52,847,051 are the largest revenue item. Expenses total includes depreciation of $5,213,497 and business profit distributions of $4,313,000 as 'other items' below the operating surplus line. Total expenses per statement = $42,012,306 operating + $9,526,497 other items = $51,538,803. OSR calculated as total revenue minus ISC ($22,744,263), Health Canada ($2,494,586), CMHC ($191,609), First Nations Development Funding ($130,000), and Athabasca Tribal Council ($304,720) = total government transfers $25,865,178; however long-term sustainability funding and industry grants ($30,072,450) appears to be largely from industry not government so excluded from government transfers calculation. No schedule of remuneration included in the financial statements. Prior year accumulated surplus was $260,339,330 per the statement but $315,292,959 is the 2021 closing balance shown on the balance sheet — the $260,339,330 is the 2021 opening balance (i.e., March 31, 2020 balance).
